Make a Difference

Help Keep Girls in School.

One donation. One pad. One girl who gets to stay in class today. Your generosity creates ripples that last a lifetime.

₦2,500

provides sanitary pads for one girl

₦10,000

supports multiple girls through an outreach

₦50,000

helps sponsor a full school outreach

Secure Donation

Make Your Donation

Choose an amount below or enter your own. All donations are processed securely via Flutterwave.

Thank you for your donation! Your contribution of will help keep a girl in school. We'll send a confirmation to your email.
SSL Encrypted
Secure Payment
Flutterwave Verified
Your Impact

Where Your Money Goes

Every naira donated goes directly to purchasing sanitary products, funding outreach programs, and delivering menstrual health education to girls who need it most.

₦2,500

Provides a month's supply of sanitary pads for one girl, keeping her in class every day of the month.

₦10,000

Supports multiple girls through a single outreach visit, including pads, education materials, and hygiene kits.

₦50,000

Sponsors a full school outreach program — reaching an entire classroom with products, education, and awareness.

₦200,000+

Funds a multi-school community drive reaching hundreds of girls across an entire community in one day.

Why Donors Trust Us

  • 100% of donations fund direct programs
  • Transparent reporting on impact
  • Verified nonprofit organization
  • Donation receipts provided
More Ways to Help

Other Ways to Give

Can't donate right now? There are many other meaningful ways to support our mission.

Volunteer

Join our team of passionate volunteers and make a direct impact through community outreach and school programs.

Learn More

Partner

Align your brand with a cause that matters. Corporate partnerships amplify our reach and your social impact.

Partner With Us

Share

Spread the word on social media. Awareness is free and can inspire others to give, volunteer, or donate pads.

Follow & Share

Ending period poverty and empowering every girl's potential through menstrual hygiene support, education, and advocacy.
